intro: NEXPLANON is covered by most healthcare plans. If covered, it will be covered as either a medical benefit or a pharmacy benefit — and that distinction determines how you order it. The Organon Access Program can help determine specific coverage, or your office (or the patient) can verify with the plan directly.

benefitChoices.label: Medical benefit
benefitChoices.blurb: The drug and the procedure are covered under the same benefit. NEXPLANON is ordered through a specialty distributor (buy & bill): your practice buys the implant and bills the insurer for both the implant and the procedure.

benefitChoices.label: Pharmacy benefit
benefitChoices.blurb: The drug is covered separately from the procedure. Routes to the Assignment of Benefits (AOB) process through a specialty pharmacy, which ships the product to your office and directly bills the medical insurer.

benefitChoices.label: Not sure — help me verify
benefitChoices.blurb: Get the payer-call script or have the Organon Access Program run a benefit investigation.

callScript.heading: Verify coverage with the plan
steps: Call the customer service number listed on the back of the health insurance card.
Tell the representative that NEXPLANON is a provider-administered birth control drug, and that the provider will prescribe, order, and insert NEXPLANON.
Ask the representative if NEXPLANON is covered and, if not, ask if there is coverage as a pharmacy benefit.
questionsIfCovered: Are NEXPLANON and the insertion procedure 100% covered? If not, what are the out-of-pocket costs?
Is the removal procedure for NEXPLANON also covered? If not, what are the out-of-pocket costs?

costNote: The cost of NEXPLANON depends on a patient's insurance plan. Some patients may have coverage with out-of-pocket expenses, including a deductible, co-payment, or other charges, and may pay additional charges in connection with insertion and/or removal (e.g., office visit co-pay). Confirm each patient's coverage and cost-sharing obligations with the relevant payer.

medicaid.heading: Medicaid
medicaid.note: As with private payers, verify coverage for patients on Medicaid prior to initiating treatment.
points: State Medicaid programs offer coverage for contraceptives, but eligibility guidelines may vary.
Among women enrolled in Medicaid managed-care organizations, coverage will be based on the specific policies of that managed-care organization.

investigation.heading: Have the Organon Access Program verify coverage for you
investigation.body: The Organon Access Program can conduct a benefit investigation to determine how NEXPLANON is covered for your patient and which billing codes to use.
